title: silene bhutanica
intro: Silene bhutanica, also known as Bhutanian catchfly, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Silene bhutanica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
